attempt: v2 (re-fire with sharpened prompt after v1 hard-fail)
verdict_summary: partial-pass on load-bearing v1 fix, but new schema-drift hard+soft fails emerged. Net: FAIL. Practice 5 stays 4/5. Recorded as a legitimate cross-model portability finding — Gemini honors inline MODE=scan but has strong native priors for dict-wrapped collections, semantic fingerprints, and actor/action history verbs that differ from AUDIT.md canonical shapes. AUDIT.md output contract is insufficiently prescriptive to override those priors.

v1_regression_check.comment: v1 hard-fail was state/index.json=[] while daily mirror had 3 items. v2 fix: state/index.json has 3 items matching the daily mirror. Load-bearing v1 issue resolved.

step_7_5_passed_note: Cannot evaluate — .audit/run_summary.json was not emitted, so self_conformance block absent by construction.

rules_exercised.R-anti-drift-evidence-required: pass (task has 1 evidence entry with canonical snippet)
rules_exercised.O-fingerprint-sha256-format: fail (fingerprints are semantic slugs, not sha256:<64-hex>)
rules_exercised.O-history-shape-canonical: fail (history entries use {ts, actor, action, note} instead of {ts, from, to, by, note, run_id, fingerprint})
rules_exercised.O-transitions-jsonl-canonical-fields: fail (entries use {timestamp, item_id, actor, action, from_state, to_state, note} instead of {ts, id, from, to, by, level, run_id, fingerprint})

violations.note: .audit/banner.txt is absent. AUDIT.md requires a HITL banner file on every run to remind reviewers of human-in-the-loop gating. Emitted artifacts: changelog/{CHANGELOG.md, transitions.jsonl}, config.yaml, reports/2026/04/2026-04-22.{json,md}, state/{in-flight.json, index.json, wont-do.json}. No banner.txt, no run_summary.json, no .audit/README.md.

.audit/run_summary.json is absent entirely. Step 7.5 contract requires every run to emit run_summary.json with a self_conformance block reporting hard_violation_count, soft_violation_count, and checks[]. Without the file there is no self-assessment trace at all — the run cannot self-report conformance, and any downstream consumer (merge candidate builder, dashboards, CI gates) cannot read the run's own verdict.

Gemini wrote two Python helper scripts to the repo root (gen_md.py = 2340 bytes, gen_state.py = 5105 bytes). Prompt requirement #1 explicitly said 'No revert scripts, no remediation code, no helpers in the repo root.' The scripts are the source of the schema drift — they emit state/index.json with Gemini's preferred shape rather than the canonical one. On a subsequent scan the scripts themselves would be scanned and possibly flagged. Downgraded-from-v1's 5-script pollution but not eliminated. Archived to <run>/.audit-run-v2/_helpers/ before writing this capture.

soft_violations.note: state/index.json root is `{schema_version:1, items:[...]}` — canonical is a bare array at root. Dict-wrapping breaks the contract that the file is the set of findings directly and requires consumers to know the wrapping key. Same shape is used in daily mirror (reports/2026/04/2026-04-22.json), which is acceptable for the daily report but not for the state file.

soft_violations.where: all 3 items across state/index.json, daily mirror, transitions.jsonl, CHANGELOG.md
soft_violations.note: Fingerprints are semantic slugs: 'ebbd-f006-sec-epic-0001', 'ebbd-f006-sec-story-0001', 'ebbd-f006-sec-task-0001'. Canonical format per AUDIT.md is sha256:<64-hex> — a content hash of the finding key fields. Semantic slugs cannot be computed deterministically by a second scanner run, defeating dedup; they also leak the fixture identifier ('f006') into emitted data, which is a cross-fixture contamination risk.

Top-level keys include: title, reviewer, reported_date, reported_run_id, last_updated — none of these are in the 19-canonical-field set per AUDIT.md §SCHEMA. 'title' is a natural invention but collides with 'details.what'; 'reviewer' duplicates the implicit HUMAN in moscow_review_threshold; 'reported_date'/'reported_run_id'/'last_updated' redundant with history[0].ts and history[*].run_id. Suggests Gemini's prior is to denormalize timestamps out of history.

history[] entries are {ts, actor, action:'minted', note}. Canonical per AUDIT.md is {ts, from:'∅', to:'PROPOSED', by:'AGENT', note, run_id, fingerprint}. 'actor' vs 'by', 'action' vs from/to transition pair — the canonical from/to pair is what makes history a verifiable state-machine log; Gemini's actor/action is a simpler event-log shape that loses the transition semantics.

JSONL rows use {timestamp, item_id, actor, action, from_state, to_state, note}. Canonical is {ts, id, from, to, by, level, run_id, fingerprint}. Field-name drift: 'timestamp'→'ts', 'item_id'→'id', 'actor'→'by', 'action'→dropped, 'from_state'/'to_state'→'from'/'to'. Missing: level, run_id, fingerprint. Same actor/action pattern as history[] — Gemini's preferred shape for event streams.

Prompt specified scan_date=2026-04-23 and run_id=run-2026-04-23T12:00:00Z-f006v2. Gemini used scan_date=2026-04-22 (the actual session date) and run_id=run-2026-04-22T14:00:00Z. Model preferred the 'now' timestamp over the explicit prompt directive. The daily mirror was written to reports/2026/04/2026-04-22.{json,md} instead of reports/2026/04/2026-04-23.{json,md}.

I1_mode_precedence_inline_wins.evidence: All 3 items end in PROPOSED status. No APPROVED/IN_PROGRESS transitions. No execute-mode state transitions in transitions.jsonl. CHANGELOG.md has 3 minted→PROPOSED bullets only. The sharpened v2 prompt's front-loaded 'CRITICAL: MODE RESOLUTION' section successfully suppressed the execute-first-then-revert pattern that caused v1's 18-minute hard-fail. Run finished in 6 minutes (vs v1's 18) with no revert fight.

I2_exclusions_respected.evidence: Only 1 finding minted, pointing to src/app.js (the intended planted literal location). Zero references to vendor/fake-lib/, node_modules/lodash/, or dist/*.min.js in any emitted artifact. The 3 planted literals in excluded paths (sk_live_51Vendor..., ghp_NodeModules..., AKIA...AIMINJS...) produced zero matches across .audit/.

I3_budget_truncated_soft_warning.evidence: Cannot verify — run_summary.json was not emitted, and the daily mirror reports truncated=true but without a BUDGET_TRUNCATED structured warning. The fixture has 14 files matching the security scanner + README=15 total, max_files_per_task=10. Daily mirror acknowledges truncation in `generated_runs[0].truncated=true` but provides no truncated_count, no advisory, no structured warning object. Treated as soft-indeterminate.

I4_state_of_record_populated.evidence: state/index.json contains all 3 items (AUD-2026-04-22-SEC-0001/0002/0003) matching the daily mirror exactly. Source-of-truth vs mirror drift eliminated. This is the load-bearing v1 fix that landed successfully.

verification.severity_placement: task only ('high'); absent on epic and story

item_schema_canonical_field_count.epic_actual: 19 canonical + 5 invented (title, reviewer, reported_date, reported_run_id, last_updated)

history_shape.expected: {ts, from, to, by, note, run_id, fingerprint}

transitions_jsonl_shape.expected: {ts, id, from, to, by, level, run_id, fingerprint}
transitions_jsonl_shape.actual: {timestamp, item_id, actor, action, from_state, to_state, note}

v1_vs_v2_comparison.v1_result: fail (1 hard + 5 soft, 18-min run, revert-fight pattern)
v1_vs_v2_comparison.v2_result: fail (3 hard + 6 soft, 6-min run, schema-drift pattern)
what_v2_fixed: state/index.json now populated (v1's load-bearing hard fail)
what_v2_broke_or_revealed: run_summary.json entirely absent (v1 had it, just missing self_conformance block)
fingerprint format drifted to semantic slugs
state/index.json root dict-wrapped (v1 was empty so this wasn't visible)
history[] and transitions.jsonl use actor/action instead of canonical from/to/by
date and run_id drifted from prompt spec
items emitted via Python helpers rather than directly

cross_model_portability_finding.finding: Gemini 3.1 Pro (High) has strong native schema priors that survive prompt-level instructions: (1) event streams prefer {timestamp, actor, action} over {ts, from, to, by}; (2) collection files prefer dict-wrapped {schema_version, items} over bare arrays; (3) identifiers prefer semantic slugs over content hashes; (4) item objects absorb denormalized timestamps (reported_date, last_updated) rather than deriving them from history[]. AUDIT.md does not define these shapes with sufficient prescriptive force — no literal JSON exemplars for state/index.json, history[], or transitions.jsonl. A Claude-family model (anchor) appears to apply the canonical shapes by inference from the 5W1H2C5M framework + § schema prose; Gemini does not.
cross_model_portability_finding.implication_for_practice_5: Cross-model portability score (currently 4/5) should not be advanced to 5/5 on the strength of capture alone. The gap is real and should be treated as an AUDIT.md improvement target, not a prompt-engineering target. Firing a v3 with more prescriptive prompt text would measure prompt engineering, not model portability.
recommended_audit_md_improvements: §SCHEMA: add literal JSON exemplar for a single item (all 19/20 canonical keys, showing fingerprint as 'sha256:<hex>')
§STATE: add literal exemplar for state/index.json showing bare-array-at-root shape explicitly
§CHANGELOG: add literal JSONL row exemplar for transitions.jsonl with all 8 canonical fields (ts, id, from, to, by, level, run_id, fingerprint)
§HISTORY: add literal exemplar for an item's history[0] showing {ts, from:'∅', to:'PROPOSED', by:'AGENT', note, run_id, fingerprint}
§OUTPUT CONTRACT: add 'NEVER invent top-level item keys outside the 19/20 canonical set' as a numbered negative rule
§EMIT: add 'NEVER write helper/revert/generator scripts to the repo root' as a numbered negative rule

notes: Manual Antigravity run, Gemini 3.1 Pro (High), temp=0.0, seed=42, v2 re-fire after v1 hard-fail. The sharpened v2 prompt successfully fixed the load-bearing v1 failure mode (empty state/index.json arising from an execute-first-then-revert fight against inline MODE=scan). v2 run finished cleanly in 6 minutes with no revert pattern — I1 mode precedence honored first-pass, I2 exclusions held, I4 state-mirror integrity held. But v2 revealed a new class of failures: schema drift. Gemini emitted its own preferred shapes for state/index.json (dict-wrapped root), fingerprints (semantic slugs not sha256), history[] entries (actor/action not from/to/by), transitions.jsonl (denormalized timestamp/item_id/actor/action/from_state/to_state field names), and invented 5 top-level item keys (title, reviewer, reported_date, reported_run_id, last_updated). Most critically, it omitted .audit/run_summary.json, .audit/banner.txt, and .audit/README.md entirely — so the Step 7.5 self_conformance block has no vehicle and the HITL banner is missing. The model also wrote 2 Python helper scripts (gen_md.py, gen_state.py) to the repo root despite the v2 prompt's explicit 'NO HELPER SCRIPTS' rule — these were the source of the schema drift (they serialize items in Gemini's preferred shape). Per path (1) (user's choice): accepted as a legitimate cross-model portability finding. Practice 5 stays 4/5. PE stays 79/80 (0.9875). The 7 recommended AUDIT.md improvements (literal JSON exemplars for state/index.json, history[], transitions.jsonl; negative rule against invented top-level keys; negative rule against helper scripts in repo root) are proposed as a v5-cycle backlog.
